title: Add Linux Root Propagation to kpod create and run
body:
Add [r]shared, [r]private, [r]slave functionality to the --volume flag
for kpod create and kpod run
This sets the root propagation for each bind mount

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Code LGTM, but you (or maybe @mheon?) have whitespace issues.
```
 * e1311ea "Merge pull request #51 from mheon/sql_state_tests" ... FAIL
  - FAIL - has whitespace errors. See `git show --check e1311ea722e26e3680af411876a43bbe85ccf83f`.
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat git validation appears to be failing for valid commits. We're probably going to stop running it on Travis - it works fine in the Papr tests.
@mheon OK thanks.  Now that you said that, did we just change that in CRIO recently?  Probably makes sense to yank it.
@mheon thanks for the info, I thought that was a flake so kept retesting it.
@umohnani8 #91 just merged, so gitvalidation is gone - rebase and repush and the tests should be green
@rhatdan One more question, and then you can LGTM and merge

:pushpin: Commit df600b8 has been approved by `rhatdan`
